page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is a storage-optimized server powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. This ARM64-based instance features 2 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 12.0 GB of memory, establishing a 1:6 vCPU-to-memory ratio. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The system runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates that the is4gen.large delivers average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1519.59. However, multi-core and memory performance metrics are generally low. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is weak at 3013.58, and the Geekbench multi-core score is poor at 1753.0. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 37.37 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 18.32 GB/sec. Redis SET operations reach 2.41 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 2.34 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its high local storage density contrasted with its modest computational and memory bandwidth capabilities. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server provides a high volume of local NVMe SSD storage per vCPU, making it a specialized option for storage-intensive workloads that do not require intense CPU or memory throughput. Appropriate workloads include local database hosting, low-throughput file serving, and basic storage-heavy applications.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is an ARM64 storage-optimized server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores at 2.5 GHz, 12.0 GB of RAM, and a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD. While the instance offers a high storage-to-vCPU ratio, benchmarks show modest compute and memory performance, with a weak stress-ng multi-core score of 3013.58 and a poor Geekbench multi-core score of 1753.0. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large block read speeds at 37.37 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy workloads requiring high local capacity but low computational intensity, such as lightweight database hosting and file serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is an ARM64 storage-optimized server featuring 2 Graviton physical cores, 12 GB of RAM, and a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD designed for storage-intensive workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring a high-capacity local NVMe SSD and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio for storage-intensive applications.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores
1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data needs
12.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:6 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight database hosting and storage-heavy workloads
